title: ROMAN TUNNELS
content_html:
Roman tunnels

The Romans, who once controlled areas of Europe, Africa and Asia Minor, adopted the construction techniques of other civilizations to build tunnels in their territories

The Persians, who lived in present-day Iran, were one of the first civilizations to build tunnels that provided a reliable supply of water to human settlements in dry areas. In the early first millennium BCE, they introduced the qanat method of tunnel construction, which consisted of placing posts over a hill in a straight line, to ensure that the tunnel kept to its route, and then digging vertical shafts down into the ground at regular intervals. Underground, workers removed the earth from between the ends of the shafts, creating a tunnel. The excavated soil was taken up to the surface using the shafts, which also provided ventilation during the work. Once the tunnel was completed, it allowed water to flow from the top of a hillside down towards a canal, which supplied water for human use. Remarkably, some qanats built by the Persians 2,700 years ago are still in use today.

They later passed on their knowledge to the Romans, who also used the qanat method to construct water-supply tunnels for agriculture. Roma qanat tunnels were constructed with vertical shafts dug at intervals of between 30 and 60 meters. The shafts were equipped with handholds and footholds to help those climbing in and out of them and were covered with a wooden or stone lid. 4-6To ensure that the shafts were vertical, Romans hung a plumb line from a rod placed across the top of each shaft and made sure that the weight at the end of it hung in the center of the shaft. Plumb lines were also used to measure the depth of the shaft and to determine the slope of the tunnel. The 5. 6-kilometer-long Claudius tunnel, built in 41 CE to drain the Fucine Lake in central Italy, had shafts that were up to 122 meters deep, took 11 years to build and involved approximately 30,000 workers.

By the 6th century BCE, a second method of tunnel construction appeared called the counter-excavation method, in which the tunnel was constructed from both ends. It was used to cut through high mountains when the qanat method was not a practical alternative. This method required greater planning and advanced knowledge of surveying, mathematics and geometry as both ends of a tunnel had to meet correctly at the center of the mountain. Adjustments to the direction of the tunnel also had to be made whenever builders encountered geological problems or when it deviated from its set path. They constantly checked the tunnel’s advancing direction, for example, by looking back at the light that penetrated through the tunnel mouth, and made corrections whenever necessary. Large deviations could happen, and they could result in one end of the tunnel not being usabl

An inscription written on the side of a 428-meter tunnel, built by the Romans as part of the Saldae aqueduct system in modern-day Algeria, describes how the two teams of builders missed each other in the mountain and how the later construction of a lateral link between both corridors corrected the initial error. The Romans dug tunnels for their roads using the counter-excavation method, whenever they encountered obstacles such as hills or mountains that were too high for roads to pass over. An example is the 37-meter-long, 6-meter-high, Furlo Pass Tunnel built in Italy in 69-79 CE. Remarkably, a modern road still uses this tunnel today. Tunnels were also built for mineral extraction. Miners would locate a mineral vein and then pursue it with shafts and tunnels underground. Traces of such tunnels used to mine gold can still be found at the Dolaucothi mines in Wales. When the sole purpose of a tunnel was mineral extraction, construction required less planning, as the tunnel route was determined by the mineral vein.

Roman tunnel projects were carefully planned and carried out. The length of time it took to construct a tunnel depended on the method being used and the type of rock being excavated. The qanat construction method was usually faster than the counter-excavation method as it was more straightforward. This was because the mountain could be excavated not only from the tunnel mouths but also from shafts. The type of rock could also influence construction times. When the rock was hard, the Romans employed a technique called fire quenching which consisted of heating the rock with fire, and then suddenly cooling it with cold water so that it would crack. Progress through hard rock could be very slow, and it was not uncommon for tunnels to take years, if not decades, to be built. Construction marks left on a Roman tunnel in Bologna show that the rate of advance through solid rock was 30 centimeters per day. In contrast, the rate of advance of the Claudius tunnel can be calculated at 1. 4 meters per day. Most tunnels had inscriptions showing the names of patrons who ordered construction and sometimes the name of the architect. For example, the 1. 4-kilometer Çevlik tunnel in Turkey, built to divert the floodwater threatening the harbor of the ancient city of Seleuceia Pieria, had inscriptions on the entrance, still visible today, that also indicate that the tunnel was started in 69 CE and was completed in 81 CE.

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–6

Complete the notes below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
ensure tunnel kept to its route
posts over a hill
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 1: “placing posts over a hill in a straight line”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Diagram hỏi vật được đặt trên đồi để giữ đúng hướng.
Passage cho biết đó là “posts”.
=> Đáp án: posts

_blockConfig.noteContent:
- [1] to direct the tunnelling
- water runs into a [2] used by local people
- vertical shafts to remove earth and for [3] 
- [4] made of wood or stone
- [5] attached to the plumb line
- handholds and footholds used for [6]

questions.questionHtml:
• 1_______ to direct the tunnelling
  • water runs into a 2_______ used by local people
  • vertical shafts to remove earth and for 3_______
  • 4_______ made of wood or stone
  • 5_______ attached to the plumb line
  • handholds and footholds used for 6_______

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
water flowed towards a …
supplied water for human use
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 1: “down towards a canal”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Nước chảy xuống một nơi dẫn nước cho con người sử dụng.
Từ đúng trong passage là “canal”.
=> Đáp án: canal

Bước 1: Keyword
shafts also provided …
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 1: “the shafts … also provided ventilation during the work”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Diagram hỏi chức năng của shafts trong lúc xây tunnel.
Passage cho biết chúng dùng để thông gió.
=> Đáp án: ventilation

Bước 1: Keyword
made of wood or stone
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 2: “were covered with a wooden or stone lid”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Diagram hỏi vật che phía trên shaft.
Passage gọi đó là “lid”.
=> Đáp án: lid

Bước 1: Keyword
attached to the plumb line
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 2: “the weight at the end of it”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Vật treo ở cuối plumb line là “weight”.
Diagram cũng chỉ đúng vật đó.
=> Đáp án: weight

Bước 1: Keyword
handholds and footholds used for …
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 2: “to help those climbing in and out of them”
Bước 3: Phân tích
“climbing in and out” = leo lên xuống shaft.
Sau “used for” cần danh từ/V-ing.
Từ phù hợp là “climbing”.
=> Đáp án: climbing

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 7–10

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 7–10 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
completely replaced
qanat method
6th century BCE
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 3: “By the 6th century BCE, a second method of tunnel construction appeared called the counter-excavation method…”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Passage chỉ nói “a second method appeared” = xuất hiện thêm một phương pháp mới.
Không hề nói phương pháp mới “completely replaced” (= hoàn toàn thay thế) qanat method.
Thông tin bị trái nghĩa do thêm ý “completely replaced”.
=> Đáp án: FALSE

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: The counter-excavation method completely replaced the qanat method in the 6th century BCE.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
only experienced builders
counter-excavation method
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 3: “This method required greater planning and advanced knowledge of surveying, mathematics and geometry…”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Đoạn văn chỉ nói phương pháp này cần “advanced knowledge”.
Không có thông tin nào nói “only experienced builders were employed”.
Passage không đề cập đối tượng công nhân hay kinh nghiệm của họ.
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN
questions.question_text: Only experienced builders were employed to construct a tunnel using the counter-excavation method.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
problem
Saldae aqueduct system
ancient book
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 4: “An inscription written on the side of a 428-meter tunnel…”
Bước 3: Phân tích
“inscription” = chữ khắc trên tường/đá.
Statement lại nói thông tin được tìm thấy trong “an ancient book”.
Hai thông tin khác nhau hoàn toàn.
=> Đáp án: FALSE
questions.question_text: The information about a problem that occurred during the construction of the Saldae aqueduct system was found in an ancient book.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
mistake
two parts of the tunnel
failed to meet
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 4: “the two teams of builders missed each other in the mountain…”
Bước 3: Phân tích
“missed each other” = hai phía đào không gặp nhau.
Paraphrase thành “failed to meet”.
Ý nghĩa trùng khớp hoàn toàn.
=> Đáp án: TRUE
questions.question_text: The mistake made by the builders of the Saldae aqueduct system was that the two parts of the tunnel failed to meet.

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 11–13

Complete the notes below.

Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
Dolaucothi mines
Wales
extract
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn 4: “Traces of such tunnels used to mine gold can still be found at the Dolaucothi mines in Wales.”
Bước 3: Phân tích
“mine gold” = khai thác vàng.
Câu hỏi hỏi loại khoáng sản được khai thác.
=> Đáp án: gold

_blockConfig.noteContent:
What type of mineral were the Dolaucothi mines in Wales built to extract? 
[11]
In addition to the patron, whose name might be carved onto a tunnel? 
[12]
What part of Seleuceia Pieria was the Çevlik tunnel built to protect? 
[13]

questions.questionHtml:
What type of mineral were the Dolaucothi mines in Wales built to extract?

11_______

In addition to the patron, whose name might be carved onto a tunnel?

12_______

What part of Seleuceia Pieria was the Çevlik tunnel built to protect?

13_______
questions.question_text: What type of mineral were the Dolaucothi mines in Wales built to extract?

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
in addition to the patron
name
carved onto a tunnel
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn cuối: “Most tunnels had inscriptions showing the names of patrons ... and sometimes the name of the architect.”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Ngoài patron thì còn có “the architect”.
“carved onto a tunnel” = inscriptions.
=> Đáp án: architect
questions.question_text: In addition to the patron, whose name might be carved onto a tunnel?

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
Çevlik tunnel
protect
Seleuceia Pieria
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn cuối: “built to divert the floodwater threatening the harbor of the ancient city of Seleuceia Pieria”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Tunnel được xây để bảo vệ “the harbor”.
“protect” = “threatening”.
=> Đáp án: harbor
questions.question_text: What part of Seleuceia Pieria was the Çevlik tunnel built to protect?
